other_observers: None. I went to get Lisa, but the bird was gone by the time we returned.; John J. Rawinski found the bird. No one else saw it.
specific_location: at residence at 239 Cotten Lane, 2 miles south of Monte Vista on Hwy 15 and 1/2 mile east. Then .25 mile north to our residence.
description: As I was walking around our property trail, I noticed a bird feeding in our tree. I was very close to the tree, only a few feet away, when I saw a small warbler, about 4.5-5.0 inches long. It had yellow underparts and an olive upper side. Face was yellow and I noticed a distinct dark eyeline and a black moustacial streak coming from near the bill that looped under the eye. Crown was olive-yellow. Bill was narrow and warbler-like and dark. Neck, throat, breast and undersides were yellow and I noticed the very distinct bold, dark streaks on the sides and flanks of the bird. Tail and wings were brownish. Wingbars were indistinct. The bird was gleaning small insects attracted to the flowers of the tree.Behaviors: Gleaning insects within the tree interior. It was 82 degrees and so the bird seemed to stay in the interior of the 15 foot tall tree.Call: None heardPlumage: Breeding
